skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction using Tavily and Exa APIs through the inference.sh CLI. Supports searching, extracting, and answering from live web data.
skill.summary: Search the web, extract content, and get answers using Tavily and Exa APIs via the belt CLI.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: All 39 static analysis findings were evaluated as false positives. The 25 shell backtick detections match markdown code fence examples showing belt CLI usage, not executable backtick injections. The 4 weak cryptographic algorithm detections have no basis in the file content. The 10 hardcoded URL findings are either placeholder domains (example.com) or legitimate documentation links. No prompt injection or data exfiltration was detected. The skill does use the belt CLI and network access by transparent design, which is expected for a web search tool.

medium_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution in Code Fences (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 25 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution pattern. All instances occur inside markdown code fence blocks (```bash) where belt CLI commands are shown as usage examples. These are documentation, not executable backtick injections. The skill legitimately declares allowed-tools: Bash(belt *) for its intended web search functionality.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are inside markdown fenced code blocks showing belt CLI usage patterns. No backtick command injection syntax is present outside of code fences.

medium_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 4 instances of weak cryptographic algorithms at lines 3, 31, 38, and 150. These lines contain YAML frontmatter, markdown table headers, and a documentation link respectively. No cryptographic algorithm references exist in the file content. This is a static analyzer misdetection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of all four flagged lines confirms zero cryptographic content. The static analyzer likely matched a false pattern in non-cryptographic text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 hardcoded URLs. Three are placeholder example.com URLs used in usage examples. Two reference inference.sh infrastructure for CLI installation and an image. Three link to legitimate documentation pages. None are exfiltration endpoints or malicious domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs verified by manual review - example.com are placeholder domains, inference.sh links are the skill provider's own infrastructure and documentation.

content.user_title: Search the web and extract content with AI
content.value_statement: AI tools cannot access current web information on their own. This skill searches the web using Tavily and Exa APIs through the belt CLI.
actual_capabilities: Search the web using Tavily and Exa for current information
Extract clean text content from any public web page URL
Get direct factual answers to questions with Exa Answer
Combine web search with LLM analysis for automated research workflows
Pipe search results into RAG pipelines and downstream tools
limitations: Requires the belt CLI to be installed and configured on your system
Requires an inference.sh account with active API access
Search quality depends on Tavily and Exa service availability
Cannot search private, authenticated, or paywalled web pages

use_cases.title: Research Assistance
use_cases.description: Gather and summarize current information on any topic from live web sources.

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking with Sources
use_cases.description: Verify claims by searching the web and retrieving original source content.

use_cases.title: RAG Pipeline Integration
use_cases.description: Feed current web data into retrieval-augmented generation workflows for up-to-date responses.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Web Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Searching for information on a topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for the latest developments in renewable energy technology.

prompt_templates.title: Extract and Summarize
prompt_templates.scenario: Reading content from a specific article URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the text content from this URL and summarize the key points: https://example.com/article

prompt_templates.title: Fact-Checking Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Verifying a claim with source citations
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web to verify this claim: Global temperatures rose 1.5 degrees in 2024. Show the sources you find.

prompt_templates.title: Research Pipeline with Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Automated multi-step research with LLM analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for quantum computing breakthroughs in 2025. Extract content from the top results. Then analyze and summarize the key findings.

output_examples.input: Search the web for best practices in prompt engineering.
output_examples.output:
Here are the key findings from my web search:

1. Clear Instructions: Be specific about format, length, and style.
2. Role Prompting: Assigning a role improves response quality.
3. Chain of Thought: Step-by-step reasoning improves accuracy.
4. Few-Shot Examples: Examples of desired output help consistency.

Sources: Prompt Engineering Guide by DAIR AI, Research paper by Wei et al.

output_examples.input: Extract the main content from https://example.com/tech-article and summarize it.
output_examples.output:
I extracted the article and created this summary:

Title: The Future of Edge Computing
Key Points:
- Edge processing will reach 75 percent of enterprise data by 2026
- Latency reduced by 60 percent compared to cloud-only systems
- Major investments from AWS, Microsoft, and Google

The article was 1,247 words with three case studies.

best_practices: Use specific, detailed search queries to get more relevant results from Tavily and Exa.
Combine search with content extraction to gather full context from source pages.
Verify critical information by running multiple searches and comparing results.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad single-word queries. They return low-quality search results.
Do not extract content from pages without verifying the source is trustworthy.
Do not rely on a single search result. Cross-reference information from multiple sources.

faq.question: What services does this skill use for web search?
faq.answer: This skill uses Tavily and Exa APIs through the inference.sh CLI.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the belt CLI tool and have an inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can this skill search any website?
faq.answer: It searches publicly available web pages. It cannot access private or authenticated content.

faq.question: How is this different from Claude built-in web search?
faq.answer: This skill uses Tavily and Exa for specialized search, extraction, and direct answers.

faq.question: Can I use search results in my own applications?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pipe search results into other tools for RAG pipelines and analysis.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill is free, but it requires a belt CLI subscription with inference.sh.
